violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Ice build up in the walk-in freezer is getting excessive. Ice is building up on boxes and in one case of chicken wing parts.

Reduced-oxygen packaging. — --Establishment using reduced-oxygen process was not properly labeling the food packages. -"1/2 Beef Roast", "Beef Brisket", and "Mrs. Gerrys" products packaged by the establishment were not date marked with a package-on date or sell-by/expiration date.

General equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of equipment did not meet a design characteristic specified in the food service code. -The wire racks found under the dairy cooling unit are beginning to rust and should be replaced soon.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. -Plastic square food storage containers are stacked upright under the counter with the conveyer oven and food prep. Advise storing these upside down to prevent food particles from falling in.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -The drop ceiling tiles in the meat department are constructed of porous acoustical material. This should be the same non-porous material found in the Hot Stuff food prep area.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food * (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Repackaged potato salads in the display case were dated November 21, 2025 and November 28, 2025. This exceeds the 10 day limit allowed in food code.

Reduced-oxygen packaging. — --Establishment using reduced-oxygen process was not properly labeling the food packages. Packages of sliced and block cheeses in the display case were dated December 7, 25. This exceeds the 30 days allowed for vacuum sealed products.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Black foam meat trays were stored on the floor in the meat department.--Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Food containers and pizza boxes on the shelf below the pizza oven were not inverted.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
